methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xiongyi Cui and Linxi Fan. The valuation universe comprises 9 peers across the Robotics & Manufacturing sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but insufficient data density within narrow filters—zero same-stage matches, zero adjacent-stage matches, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches were identified. The requirement for a minimum of three comparable datapoints within tightly defined parameters was not met. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material measurement error given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and development stages represented in the broader peer set.

Without sufficient narrow-set triangulation, this analysis cannot responsibly support a specific valuation range. Further work would require either (1) expanding stage or subsector definitions with documented rationale for comparability, (2) obtaining additional transaction data from the identified peer set, or (3) applying alternative methods such as revenue multiple analysis or DCF modeling where financial performance data is available. The key uncertainty remains the absence of a statistically meaningful peer cluster at the seed stage within this sector.
